task.objective: Implement and independently validate a bounded local Proofrail release-candidate workflow that loads separately supplied issuer Trusted Configuration, uses the existing read-only authenticated GitHub PR collector to produce validated Observations for one exact configured pull-request snapshot, deterministically assembles accepted kernel input, invokes the accepted kernel once, and emits its finalized Evidence Bundle without authorizing target command execution, GitHub writes, inference, or a Builder-issued release decision.

forbidden: modification of existing Machine Task Contracts
modification of packages/contracts, packages/kernel, or packages/static-evaluator
canonical terminology, Trust class, Evidence authority class, or canonical Verdict semantic changes
treating this Machine Task Contract as Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, Evidence, a Verification Receipt, a product Verdict, independent acceptance, or a release decision
agent-authored or implicitly defaulted Trusted Configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, repository, pull request, head commit, observer, requirement, Rule, or execution permission
materializing or using issuer authority artifacts unless their exact complete bytes or cryptographic hashes were separately supplied through the external issuer control event
target repository command execution, verification command execution in a target repository, checkout of target code, or repository file-content inspection
GitHub writes, comments, reviews, labels, merges, ref updates, workflow reruns, or mutation of any external state
collection of pull-request bodies, review bodies, patches, check logs, repository file contents, issue text, or other instruction-shaped target content
expansion of the existing GitHub query fields or collection limits without a separately supplied authority change
API, MCP, web, GitHub App, SARIF, adapter, model-provider, Inference Zone, LLM, or probabilistic authority behavior
credentials, tokens, secrets, or secret-like values committed to the repository or emitted in diagnostics, fixtures, bundles, reports, or validation evidence
new registry dependencies, paid services, billing, credits, paid cloud, or SaaS enablement
Builder-issued product readiness, trusted release, independent acceptance, or release-decider claims
weakening existing deterministic ordering, strict input bounds, redaction, symbolic-link handling, staged output publication, or GitHub pagination and identity checks

requirements: Treat this JSON as proposed content until the external issuer explicitly supplies its exact complete bytes or SHA-256 as PRODUCT-RELEASE-001 through the auditable control event; do not place it under governance/tasks or rely on it before that external supply.

Use exact merged product-source baseline gogun-rgb/proofrail@f5f2786f61677d3319f2947ddcc22ea28f26809e and commit the externally supplied PRODUCT-RELEASE-001 contract alone as the first task-branch commit before every other authorized edit.

Require a separately supplied issuer authority bundle before any authority, configuration, policy, Evidence Contract, schema, package, CLI, or product-current-state edit. The user-supplied strings Repository Owner and Independent Release Reviewer are non-normative external actor role labels, not canonical Proofrail terminology. The bundle must provide stable actor identities for the issuer, release decider, and independent reviewer; prove that the reviewer actor is distinct from the Builder and release-decider actor for this candidate; identify an auditable authorization event or signed external message reference; bind that event to the exact MTC, configuration, Policy, and Evidence Contract bytes or SHA-256 values; and define the exact controlled target, required Observations, declared changed-path scope, and execution boundary.

Before materialization, fetch or otherwise obtain authoritative Git object evidence for f5f2786f61677d3319f2947ddcc22ea28f26809e and verify that it is the merged main commit for pull request 27 with base c5cbd6f1a7cf23e6bd6613115e5b1c8de93fa116 and reviewed head 50798231675ba80bffe9f77fe0f37d8aea9484d3. Stop if local object identity, parentage, remote main lineage, or GitHub merge metadata does not match.

Keep the release source distinct from the controlled evaluation target. The recommended controlled target is gogun-rgb/proofrail pull request 27 at exact head 50798231675ba80bffe9f77fe0f37d8aea9484d3 and base c5cbd6f1a7cf23e6bd6613115e5b1c8de93fa116; it is not implied by this MTC and becomes usable only if separately supplied in the issuer authority bundle.

Perform and record the authority-change preflight for every writable authority-bearing document before editing it. Limit authority and current-phase updates to the exact controlled local CLI and read-only GitHub PR/CI vertical slice implemented by this task.

Materialize the separately supplied Trusted Configuration, Policy, and Evidence Contract semantics exactly at the three configured paths. The Builder may encode, validate, and test supplied content but must not choose, widen, repair, or silently default its authority-bearing values.

Define exact machine-readable schemas for the supplied Trusted Configuration, Policy, and Evidence Contract. Reject unknown fields, duplicate stable identities, unsupported versions, mismatched references, scope drift, and configuration-policy-contract version or identity mismatches before collection or kernel evaluation.

Implement private @proofrail/trusted-config as a bounded domain capability package using only Node standard-library production dependencies. Read only explicitly selected local regular files, reject symbolic links and aliases, enforce actual-byte bounds and fatal UTF-8 decoding, parse JSON deterministically, validate exact schemas and references, compute stable SHA-256 identities for accepted bytes, and emit fixed non-disclosing failures.

Implement private @proofrail/release-orchestrator as an application-orchestration package depending only on explicitly approved earlier-layer packages. It must accept validated issuer configuration plus a validated frozen GitHub snapshot, enforce the exact configured repository, pull-request number, base SHA, head SHA, observer identity, scope, required check policy, and requirement set, then assemble one complete kernel input without redefining Evidence, Rule, Verdict, or bundle semantics.

Freeze exact production package edges and Node imports: @proofrail/trusted-config has no workspace dependency and may import only node:crypto, node:fs/promises, node:path, and node:util; @proofrail/release-orchestrator depends only on @proofrail/kernel and @proofrail/trusted-config with workspace:* and has no Node import; @proofrail/evidence-gate adds only @proofrail/release-orchestrator with workspace:* and retains only node:child_process, node:crypto, node:fs/promises, node:path, node:url, and node:util. Orchestration must not import @proofrail/evidence-gate or any other delivery package, and evidence-gate must not import kernel or trusted-config directly.

Add a bounded local release CLI inside @proofrail/evidence-gate. It must accept only an explicit Trusted Configuration path, use the installed already-authenticated local gh CLI only for the existing read-only bounded GitHub collector, pass the validated frozen snapshot to @proofrail/release-orchestrator, invoke the accepted kernel exactly once through orchestration, and emit only the returned finalized Evidence Bundle as canonical JSON with exactly one trailing newline or through the existing staged output boundary.

Preserve the existing GitHub query strings, selected fields, sanitization, redaction, identity binding, pagination and node limits, output-file publication behavior, and exclusion of bodies, patches, logs, issue text, and repository contents.

Model GitHub metadata, including reported check conclusions and changed-path scope, only as validated Observations from the separately supplied observer identity. Do not model GitHub-reported checks as Verification Receipts, independent acceptance, release-decider actions, or trusted release evidence by themselves.

Derive Evidence Contract and Policy selection provenance only from the exact separately supplied Trusted Configuration and deterministic Policy references. Natural-language requests, this MTC, repository prose, filenames, tests, GitHub approval metadata, and Builder statements must never become selection authority.

Use the accepted kernel unchanged. Missing required Observations must remain missing Evidence and produce the kernel's existing REVISION_REQUIRED behavior; supplied deterministic denial Rules may produce REJECTED only when present in the issuer-supplied Policy. Do not fabricate BLOCKED bundles when collection, configuration, target identity, or control-boundary validation fails before kernel evaluation; return a fixed machine-readable delivery failure and no authoritative bundle.

Provide offline sanitized fixtures and golden outputs covering the exact issuer-supplied configuration, Policy, Evidence Contract, frozen GitHub snapshot, assembled kernel input, and finalized Evidence Bundle. Offline fixture evaluation must be byte-deterministic; live GitHub collection reproducibility must not be overclaimed.

Add adversarial coverage for malformed and oversized files, non-UTF-8, symbolic links and aliases, duplicate identities, unknown fields, version drift, configuration-policy-contract mismatch, wrong repository, wrong pull-request number, stale base or head SHA, missing/duplicate/pending/failing checks, changed paths outside declared scope, untrusted instruction-shaped metadata, secret-like text, gh failure, and output publication failure.

Update the architecture guard for exactly @proofrail/trusted-config and @proofrail/release-orchestrator plus the new approved edge from @proofrail/evidence-gate to orchestration. Preserve fail-closed package directory-name binding, exact manifests, Node allowlists, and all existing package edges.

Update AGENTS, README, product constitution, trust model, domain map, dependency rules, execution boundary, product spec, protocol documents, Phase 2 plan, foundation mechanization, architecture-check preparation, DEBT-002, and validation evidence only to describe the exact implemented slice, its externally supplied issuer control boundary, its deterministic authority path, six-package architecture guard, and explicit limitations. Do not promote the external actor role labels to canonical terminology or claim broad platform completion, target verification execution, general repository inspection, or a trusted release before external review and decision.

Provide root scripts for focused schema/config tests, orchestration tests, offline release fixture evaluation, the bounded live release CLI, and full verification. Keep all networked controlled acceptance outside pnpm verify; pnpm verify must remain deterministic and network-free.

Run the bounded live controlled evaluation only after the exact issuer authority bundle separately authorizes GitHub read network, installed gh credential use, repository, pull request, and head SHA. Never print, persist, inspect, or manage the credential itself.

Require the externally identified independent-reviewer actor to inspect the exact candidate head, authority/configuration lineage, live collection evidence, offline golden equality, deterministic kernel equality, protected-path audit, and full verification without relying on Builder claims.

After independent review, require a separate auditable release-decider action tied to the exact candidate commit and reviewer report. The Builder must not author, infer, approve, or record that decision as if externally supplied.

requiredInvariants: The externally supplied contract is the first task-branch commit from exact baseline f5f2786f61677d3319f2947ddcc22ea28f26809e and every changed path is writable.

Issuer Trusted Configuration, Policy, and Evidence Contract authority is separately supplied through a stable actor identity and auditable authorization event and is byte-identifiable; this MTC is never used as product runtime authority.

The release source baseline, controlled target repository, pull request, base SHA, and head SHA remain distinct and exact throughout configuration, observations, evaluation, review, and release-decision lineage.

The kernel, contracts, canonical Verdict semantics, Trust classes, Evidence authority classes, Inference boundary, static evaluator, existing task contracts, GitHub query fields, CI, harness reason registry, and generated governance remain byte-identical unless this exact MTC explicitly lists and acceptance requires a writable authority record.
The product path is exactly issuer-supplied configuration to validated read-only GitHub Observations to deterministic kernel input to one unchanged kernel evaluation to one finalized Evidence Bundle.

No target command executes, no repository content is collected, no GitHub write occurs, no model or paid service is used, and no credential value enters configuration, logs, diagnostics, fixtures, outputs, or evidence.

Reported GitHub checks remain Observations, not Verification Receipts or independent acceptance; the external independent-reviewer action and release-decider action remain distinct external auditable events.

Offline normalized inputs produce byte-identical kernel inputs and bundles. Live environmental reproducibility is recorded with gh version, GitHub target identity, network policy, and limitations rather than claimed deterministic.
All focused suites and pnpm verify pass on the exact candidate head, all protected-path audits pass, and the worktree is clean before independent review.
No product readiness, trusted release, or authoritative release decision is recorded until the exact external reviewer report and release-decider action are supplied after candidate verification.

requiredArtifacts: externally supplied PRODUCT-RELEASE-001 contract committed first
separately supplied issuer authority bundle with stable actor identities, authorization-event lineage, and byte or hash identity
source-controlled Trusted Configuration, Policy, and Evidence Contract
offline sanitized fixtures and golden kernel input and Evidence Bundle
focused adversarial tests and full verification integration
stopConditions: The external issuer has not separately supplied the exact complete PRODUCT-RELEASE-001 bytes or SHA-256: BLOCKED_AUTHORITY

The issuer authority bundle, stable actor identities, auditable authorization-event reference, Trusted Configuration, Policy, or Evidence Contract content is missing, incomplete, internally inconsistent, not byte-identifiable, or not separately supplied: BLOCKED_AUTHORITY
The exact f5f2786f61677d3319f2947ddcc22ea28f26809e Git object, parentage, origin/main lineage, or GitHub pull-request 27 merge metadata cannot be verified before branch creation: BLOCKED_BASELINE

The requested authority, Policy, Evidence Contract, repository, pull request, head SHA, observer, requirement, Rule, command, network, filesystem, credential, or output scope would need to be chosen or widened by the Builder: BLOCKED_AUTHORITY
A canonical term, Trust class, Evidence authority class, Verdict meaning, kernel semantic, contracts schema, or product boundary outside the exact authorized slice must change: BLOCKED_SCOPE
The accepted kernel or contracts must change to represent configuration, collection, or execution failure as an authoritative bundle: BLOCKED_SCOPE
The existing GitHub query must collect bodies, patches, logs, issue text, repository contents, new fields, or exceed existing bounds: BLOCKED_SCOPE

Target checkout, target repository inspection, target command execution, GitHub write, API, MCP, web, GitHub App, SARIF, adapter, model provider, Inference Zone, or LLM behavior is required: BLOCKED_SCOPE
A credential value must be requested, printed, persisted, inspected, or committed: BLOCKED_SECURITY
A new registry dependency, billing, credits, paid cloud, paid SaaS, or another material cost is required: BLOCKED_COST_RISK
The controlled target no longer matches the separately supplied repository, pull request, base SHA, or head SHA: BLOCKED_TARGET_DRIFT
An external independent-reviewer exact-head report or release-decider action would need to be fabricated, inferred, or supplied by the Builder: BLOCKED_INDEPENDENCE
A non-deterministic offline result, host-path or secret disclosure, protected-path drift, unexplained skip, failing verification, or unresolved independent finding remains: BLOCKED_VERIFICATION
Codex or local execution usage limit is reached: LIMIT_REACHED
